commit.message:
fix(ml): preserve else-branch defaults when no original feature_context

The previous commit injected historicalOccupancy into feature_context
even when the caller passed None, causing add_bridge_day_feature to run
and crash with KeyError('date_local') — that function was never called
in the no-context path before.

Fix: only inject into feature_context when it was non-None. In the else
branch, call add_park_occupancy_feature directly with a minimal context
containing only historicalOccupancy + baseTime, leaving all other
defaults (bridge_day=0, virtual_queue=0, etc.) unchanged.

commit.message:
Remove redundant ModCommandHandlerAfter from EnableInventoryInVehicle

The override returned true for COMMANDID_MOVE on non-player entities,
which could block the local player's action processing if GetPlayer()
returned null during transitions (mag swap, reload, vehicle entry).
SurvivorAnims patch already guards AI proxies safely with return false.

commit.message:
fix: force setActive() in OrgGate when org is present but missing from JWT

When a user signs in, Clerk knows the active org client-side but the JWT
cookie may not include orgId yet. OrgGate now calls setActive() to write
the org into the JWT, ensuring server-side auth().orgId is populated
before any API fetches fire.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE for real content

When all detectors return 0 (no AI signals found), combinedAiScore=0 was
being passed as undefined, which defaulted to 50 in the engine (grey zone)
and landed in INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.

Fix: always pass the combined score. Zero = no AI evidence = Likely Original.
Also change engine fallback from 50 to 0 for the same reason.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ml): use historical occupancy profile for future prediction rows

park_occupancy_pct (15% feature importance) was broadcast from current
real-time value to ALL prediction rows including those 24h/14d in the
future, causing flat predictions with no time-of-day variation.

Fix:
- db.py: add fetch_historical_park_occupancy() - computes expected
  park occupancy by (DOW, hour) over last 8 weeks using per-ride P50
  baseline (matching TypeScript calculation), 1h cache
- predict.py: fetch historical profile once per prediction call and
  inject into feature_context before feature engineering
- features.py: add_park_occupancy_feature now applies:
  - Real-time parkOccupancy for rows within ±2h of base_time
  - Historical (DOW, hour) profile for all future rows
  - Historical profile for daily predictions with no real-time context

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix pos_weight: lower to 0.2 to improve TNR

pos_weight=1.0 was wrong direction — higher value penalises missed
positives more, driving TPR to 93% but TNR to 25%.
Lower value (0.2) makes model more conservative about predicting
positive, targeting better TNR/TPR balance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add component-wise and edit-distance second-chance name matching

When the primary 0.85 fuzzy threshold fails, two additional checks
can still merge obvious duplicates:

1. Component-wise: multi-word names sharing an exact word get a lower
   0.75 threshold on differing components (e.g., "Kenny McCulloch"
   vs "Kenny McCullough" — shared "Kenny", differ by 0.89)

2. Edit distance + length: names 8+ chars with Levenshtein distance
   <= 2 merge automatically (catches OCR typos on longer names while
   the length gate prevents "Bates"/"Gates" false positives)

commit.message:
perf(memory): reduce search latency 62% — halve rerank candidates, cap FTS fetch, shorten query ONNX

- RERANK_CANDIDATES 100→50: reranker is dominant cost (~17ms), hybrid retrieval
  already surfaces good candidates in top-50
- FTS5 fetch limit: limit*2 (200) → (limit+20).min(60), capped sparse retrieval
- ONNX query embedding: MAX_SEQ_LEN 512→128 for queries only (documents keep 512)
  via new embed_batch_inner() with configurable max_seq_len

Results: search_hybrid P50 15.4ms→5.8ms, nDCG@5 0.7520→0.7519, all 38 benchmarks pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Session 166: Smart gap classification — timing vs actionable vs irreducible

Add S3c to postPipelineAudit360.cjs that classifies orphan teams into:
- Timing: league has no recent matches (>45 days) — season transition, self-resolving
- Actionable: league HAS recent matches but orphan can't link — team resolution failure
- Irreducible: league has zero matches on platform — nothing to scrape

Update generateDashboard.cjs to display the classification breakdown:
- North Star now shows actionable/timing/irreducible counts below the total gap %
- Gap Anatomy table adds Actionable Gap, Timing Gap, Irreducible Gap rows
- Recommendation triggers on >2000 actionable orphans specifically

Current classification: 6,603 actionable (56%) | 3,950 timing (34%) | 1,201 irreducible (10%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Session 453 — slate-level observations + version test resilience

Adds observation-only slate-level tags to merged picks:
- slate_heavy_over_lean / slate_heavy_under_lean (>80% directional at 5+ picks)
- slate_same_game_same_dir (2+ picks same game same direction)
- slate_game_concentration (3+ picks from single game)

These are pure observations — no blocking. Tags attached to every selected
pick via slate_observation_tags for post-hoc analysis.

Also: relaxed version test to match 'v45' prefix (not exact version) so
version bumps don't break unrelated tests.

145 tests passing (+9 new slate observation tests).

Add 7 new features: Ops Pulse, enhanced fleet, Skills, Connection Health, Live Chat, Personality Editor, Exec Approvals

Features added:
- Ops Pulse hero on HQ: live ticker, agent heatmap with pulse rings, task flow pipeline, 24h sparklines

- Exec Approval Queue with urgency coloring, approve/deny buttons, empty state animation (DEMO data until gateway API)

- Agent Personality Editor on agent profiles: SOUL/AGENTS/USER/IDENTITY tabs, edit+preview modes, save/copy actions
- Sidebar: added CHAT 💬 and SKILLS 🧠 nav items

All gateway-dependent features use realistic mock data with TODO comments marking real API callsites.

recovery fails. This prevents users from landing on the login page
instead of being auto-redirected to their new tenancy.

- rc_image.stride changed from int32_t to uint32_t
- rc_pixel_format_bytes_per_pixel inline helper added
- rc_image_make: blank filled image, uses rc_array_bytes internally
- rc_image_make_subimage: clamped subimage view sharing parent stride

🐛 fix: inline workflow content for global installs

@-file references in Claude Code command files resolve relative to the
project root. For global installs (~/.claude), workflow files live outside
any project so all commands failed to load them. The installer now inlines
workflow content directly into command files at global install time.

Also bumps to v0.1.1.

commit.message:
refactor: rename prompt files and add extra prompt support at all levels

File naming:
- Base prompts: report_base_prompt.md, code_base_prompt.md
- Role prompts: report_prompt.md, code_prompt.md (agents), review_prompt.md,
  code_management_prompt.md (supervisor, renamed from code_management.md)
- Extra prompts: *_extra_prompt.md for each action type

Extra prompts are additive (not fallback) from up to 4 levels:
- Agent: org broad → org agent-specific → project broad → project agent-specific
- Supervisor: org supervisor/ → project supervisor/

Extra prompts are not supported in defaults/, only at org and project levels.

Prompt assembly order:
- Agent: Context → Base → Role → Extras → CLI extra
- Supervisor: Context → Action → Extras → Data → CLI extra

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove invalid --new-window Ghostty config option

Ghostty treats CLI args as config fields; --new-window is not a valid
field and causes "cli:1:new-window: unknown field" error. The -n flag
in open -na already ensures a new instance.

commit.message:
Flesh out config centralization and CI TODO items

Config centralization now describes two distinct layers: infra globals
(PROJECT/ZONE/etc) and game-specific config (WORLD_NAME, ports, paths),
with a note that per-game sourced config files are the right destination
for layer 2.

CI expanded from validate-only to two tiers: syntax/validate (free) plus
design/contract tests — shellcheck, systemd pairing checks, Requires+After
verification, template placeholder audits, shutdown sequence checks.

commit.message:
Add saved tracks dropdown with localStorage persistence (#9)

* Persist uploaded GPX files in localStorage

Store raw GPX XML in localStorage on upload so tracks survive page
reloads. Previously uploaded files are restored on DOMContentLoaded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Store uploaded GPX files in localStorage

Save raw GPX XML text and filename to localStorage on upload,
and remove the entry on track deletion to keep in sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add .beads/ to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add dropdown to select pre-stored GPX tracks

Add a <select> dropdown in #add-track that lists available GPX tracks
from two sources: server files (via /api/tracks endpoint) and localStorage
(gpxUploads key). Selecting a track loads and displays it using the same
parseGPX/dataUpdated flow as file uploads.

Server-side: add /tracks static route and /api/tracks endpoint to serve
GPX files from test_files/. Client-side: populateSavedTracks() builds
the option list, addSavedTrackListener() handles selection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix localStorage key mismatch in GPX delete handler

The delete handler in map.js used 'gpxTracks' but save/load functions
in script.js use 'gpxUploads'. This caused deleted tracks to reappear
on page reload since the wrong key was being cleared.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove server-side track fetching from saved tracks dropdown

Address review feedback: remove /api/tracks endpoint, /tracks static
route, and server track options from the dropdown. The saved tracks
dropdown now only lists tracks from localStorage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix saved tracks dropdown behavior

- Remove auto-load of tracks on page refresh (start with empty map)
- Delete button now removes track from display only, keeps in localStorage
- Dropdown refreshes when tracks are added or deleted

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review comments

- Add permanent delete option: Shift+click delete button to remove from localStorage with confirmation
- Handle localStorage quota errors: Show user-friendly alert when storage fails
- Prevent duplicate track loading: Check if track already loaded by comparing start dates
- Remove unused "local:" prefix: Prefix was added then immediately stripped
- Remove dead code: Delete loadGPXFromLocalStorage() which was never called
- Handle corrupted entries: Wrap parseGPX call in try-catch when loading from dropdown

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tests for saved tracks dropdown feature

- Add savedTracksDropdown selector
- Add seedLocalStorageNow helper for test seeding
- Add 8 tests covering:
  - Save uploaded track to localStorage
  - Populate dropdown with saved tracks
  - Load track from dropdown
  - Add uploaded track to dropdown
  - Prevent loading duplicate tracks
  - Remove track from display on delete click
  - Delete track permanently on Shift+click
  - Persist tracks across page reload

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review comments on localStorage storage

- Use unique IDs for storage entries instead of matching by filename
- Prevent bitwise duplicate storage (same GPX data), allow same filenames
- Delete by storage ID instead of matching by date
- Filter dropdown to exclude already-displayed tracks
- Track correspondence between displayed tracks and storage IDs
- Add removeTrack() function for centralized track removal
- Update tests:
  - Verify correct track loaded by checking date in legend
  - Test that loaded tracks are removed from dropdown
  - Test bitwise duplicate prevention
  - Update to use selectOption by label since value is now ID

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use content hash as storage ID

- Replace random ID generation with content hash (hashContent function)
- Content hash automatically handles duplicate detection
- Same content = same hash, so duplicates are prevented
- Update test helper to use same hash function for consistency
- Add label verification in dropdown test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use SHA-256 via WebCrypto for content hashing

- Replace simple hash with SHA-256 using crypto.subtle.digest()
- Make saveGPXToLocalStorage async to support WebCrypto
- Test helper uses Node's crypto.createHash('sha256') for same algorithm
- Remove ability to pass external IDs to seedLocalStorageNow
- IDs are always computed from content hash

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review comments: remove name field, inline hash, use track date

- Remove name field from localStorage storage (use track date instead)
- Inline SHA-256 hash code instead of separate function
- Use getStartDate(track) for dropdown text instead of filename
- Update tests to reflect new behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use useAuth().orgId instead of useOrganization() for dashboard fetch gate

useAuth().orgId directly reflects the current JWT — when non-null the server
will also see orgId via auth(). useOrganization().organization can be truthy
before the JWT has been refreshed, causing the API to still return zeros.
